title: Monroe County, New York

infobox.value: This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Monroe_County,_New_York "Special:EditPage/Monroe County, New York") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(July 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*

is a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), located along [Lake Ontario](/wiki/Lake_Ontario "Lake Ontario")'s southern shore. As of 2025, the population was 750,506, according to Census Bureau estimates.[[1]](#cite_note-QF-1) Its [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") and largest city is [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York").[[2]](#cite_note-GR6-2) The county is named after [James Monroe](/wiki/James_Monroe "James Monroe"), the fifth [president of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Monroe County is part of the [upstate](/wiki/Upstate_New_York "Upstate New York") [Rochester, NY Metropolitan Statistical Area](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York_metropolitan_area "Rochester, New York metropolitan area"). The county is part of the [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") region of the state.

**Monroe County** is a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), located along [Lake Ontario](/wiki/Lake_Ontario "Lake Ontario")'s southern shore. As of 2025, the population was 750,506, according to Census Bureau estimates.[[1]](#cite_note-QF-1) Its [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") and largest city is [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York").[[2]](#cite_note-GR6-2) The county is named after [James Monroe](/wiki/James_Monroe "James Monroe"), the fifth [president of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Monroe County is part of the [upstate](/wiki/Upstate_New_York "Upstate New York") [Rochester, NY Metropolitan Statistical Area](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York_metropolitan_area "Rochester, New York metropolitan area"). The county is part of the [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") region of the state.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

When counties were established in the province of New York in 1683, the present Monroe County was part of [Albany County](/wiki/Albany_County,_New_York "Albany County, New York"). This was an enormous county, including the northern part of the State of New York as well as all of the present State of [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont") and, in theory, extending westward to the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean"). This county was reduced in size on July 3, 1766, by the creation of [Cumberland County](/wiki/Cumberland_County,_New_York "Cumberland County, New York"), and further on March 16, 1770, by the creation of [Gloucester County](/wiki/Gloucester_County,_New_York "Gloucester County, New York"), both containing territory now in Vermont.

On March 12, 1772, what was left of Albany County was split into three parts, one remaining under the name Albany County. One of the other pieces, [Tryon County](/wiki/Tryon_County,_New_York "Tryon County, New York"), contained the western portion (and thus, since no western boundary was specified, theoretically still extended west to the Pacific). The eastern boundary of Tryon County was approximately five miles west of the present city of [Schenectady](/wiki/Schenectady,_New_York "Schenectady, New York"), and the county included the western part of the [Adirondack Mountains](/wiki/Adirondack_Mountains "Adirondack Mountains") and the area west of the West Branch of the [Delaware River](/wiki/Delaware_River "Delaware River"). The area then designated as Tryon County now includes 37 counties of the State of New York. The county was named for [William Tryon](/wiki/William_Tryon "William Tryon"), colonial governor of New York.

In the years prior to 1776, most of the Loyalists in Tryon County fled to [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"). In 1784, following the peace treaty that ended the [American Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War"), the name of Tryon County was changed to [Montgomery County](/wiki/Montgomery_County,_New_York "Montgomery County, New York") in order to honor the general, [Richard Montgomery](/wiki/Richard_Montgomery "Richard Montgomery"), who had captured several places in Canada and died attempting to capture the city of [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec_City "Quebec City"), replacing the name of the hated British governor.

In 1789, [Ontario County](/wiki/Ontario_County,_New_York "Ontario County, New York") was split off from Montgomery. The actual area split off from Montgomery County was much larger than the present county, also including the present [Allegany](/wiki/Allegany_County,_New_York "Allegany County, New York"), [Cattaraugus](/wiki/Cattaraugus_County,_New_York "Cattaraugus County, New York"), [Chautauqua](/wiki/Chautauqua_County,_New_York "Chautauqua County, New York"), [Erie](/wiki/Erie_County,_New_York "Erie County, New York"), [Genesee](/wiki/Genesee_County,_New_York "Genesee County, New York"), [Livingston](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), Monroe, [Niagara](/wiki/Niagara_County,_New_York "Niagara County, New York"), [Orleans](/wiki/Orleans_County,_New_York "Orleans County, New York"), [Steuben](/wiki/Steuben_County,_New_York "Steuben County, New York"), [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming_County,_New_York "Wyoming County, New York"), [Yates](/wiki/Yates_County,_New_York "Yates County, New York"), and part of [Schuyler](/wiki/Schuyler_County,_New_York "Schuyler County, New York") and [Wayne counties](/wiki/Wayne_County,_New_York "Wayne County, New York").

[Genesee County](/wiki/Genesee_County,_New_York "Genesee County, New York") was created by a splitting of Ontario County in 1802. This was much larger than the present Genesee County, however. It contained the present [Allegany](/wiki/Allegany_County,_New_York "Allegany County, New York"), [Cattaraugus](/wiki/Cattaraugus_County,_New_York "Cattaraugus County, New York"), [Chautauqua](/wiki/Chautauqua_County,_New_York "Chautauqua County, New York"), [Erie](/wiki/Erie_County,_New_York "Erie County, New York"), [Niagara](/wiki/Niagara_County,_New_York "Niagara County, New York"), [Orleans](/wiki/Orleans_County,_New_York "Orleans County, New York"), [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming_County,_New_York "Wyoming County, New York"), and portions of [Livingston](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") and Monroe counties.

Finally, Monroe County was formed from parts of Genesee and Ontario counties in 1821.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/U.S._Census_Bureau "U.S. Census Bureau"), the county's total area is 1,367 square miles (3,540 km2), of which 657 square miles (1,700 km2) is land and 710 square miles (1,800 km2) (52%) is water.[[4]](#cite_note-GR1-4)

Monroe County is in Western State of New York's northern tier, northeast of [Buffalo](/wiki/Buffalo,_New_York "Buffalo, New York") and northwest of [Syracuse](/wiki/Syracuse,_New_York "Syracuse, New York"). The northern county line is also the state line and the border of the United States, marked by [Lake Ontario](/wiki/Lake_Ontario "Lake Ontario"). Monroe County is north of the [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes").

## Government and politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Government and politics")]

Monroe County was chartered as a municipal corporation by the [New York State Legislature](/wiki/New_York_State_Legislature "New York State Legislature") in 1892[[5]](#cite_note-5) and rechartered under New York's Municipal Home Rule Law in 1965.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From [1856](/wiki/1856_United_States_presidential_election "1856 United States presidential election") to [1932](/wiki/1932_United_States_presidential_election "1932 United States presidential election"), Monroe County voters voted for the [Republican](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)") candidate in every presidential election apart from [1912](/wiki/1912_United_States_presidential_election "1912 United States presidential election"). [Democratic](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") candidate [Woodrow Wilson](/wiki/Woodrow_Wilson "Woodrow Wilson") was able to win the county in 1912 when the Republican vote was divided between then incumbent president [William Howard Taft](/wiki/William_Howard_Taft "William Howard Taft") and former president [Theodore Roosevelt](/wiki/Theodore_Roosevelt "Theodore Roosevelt"). Monroe County voted for incumbent Democratic presidents [Franklin D. Roosevelt](/wiki/Franklin_D._Roosevelt "Franklin D. Roosevelt") ([1936](/wiki/1936_United_States_presidential_election "1936 United States presidential election"), [1940](/wiki/1940_United_States_presidential_election "1940 United States presidential election"), [1944](/wiki/1944_United_States_presidential_election "1944 United States presidential election")) and [Harry S. Truman](/wiki/Harry_S._Truman "Harry S. Truman") ([1948](/wiki/1948_United_States_presidential_election "1948 United States presidential election")). From [1952](/wiki/1952_United_States_presidential_election "1952 United States presidential election") to [1976](/wiki/1976_United_States_presidential_election "1976 United States presidential election"), Monroe County voted for the Republican candidate in all presidential elections except for [Lyndon B. Johnson](/wiki/Lyndon_B._Johnson "Lyndon B. Johnson")'s Democratic landslide in [1964](/wiki/1964_United_States_presidential_election "1964 United States presidential election"). In [1980](/wiki/1980_United_States_presidential_election "1980 United States presidential election"), incumbent Democratic president [Jimmy Carter](/wiki/Jimmy_Carter "Jimmy Carter") won Monroe County, despite having lost in the county to Republican [Gerald Ford](/wiki/Gerald_Ford "Gerald Ford") in [1976](/wiki/1976_United_States_presidential_election "1976 United States presidential election"). Monroe County went back to voting Republican in [1984](/wiki/1984_United_States_presidential_election "1984 United States presidential election") and [1988](/wiki/1988_United_States_presidential_election "1988 United States presidential election"), but has voted for the Democratic presidential candidate every time from [1992](/wiki/1992_United_States_presidential_election "1992 United States presidential election") onwards, including the [2024](/wiki/2024_United_States_presidential_election "2024 United States presidential election") election. In 2020, [Joe Biden](/wiki/Joe_Biden "Joe Biden") received the highest percentage of the vote for a Democrat since [Lyndon Johnson](/wiki/Lyndon_B._Johnson "Lyndon B. Johnson")'s 1964 landslide.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In recent years,[*[when?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] the urban area's traditional partisan dynamic appears to have begun shifting in the [Democratic Party](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)")'s favor at the local level. A Democrat won the 2017 race for county sheriff for the first time in decades, in 2019 Democrat Adam Bello was elected county executive after over 30 years of Republican control, in 2020 democrats [Samra Brouk](/wiki/Samra_Brouk "Samra Brouk") and [Jeremy Cooney](/wiki/Jeremy_Cooney "Jeremy Cooney") flipped state senate districts long held by the GOP, and the traditionally Republican county legislature is now split 16–13 in favor of the Democratic Party. This matches a broader national trend of increased Democratic success in suburban areas.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Executive branch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Executive branch")]

The county's executive branch is headed by the [county executive](/wiki/County_executive "County executive"), Adam Bello.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) The executive's office is on the first floor of the county office building on West Main Street in Rochester. The county clerk is [Jamie Romeo](/wiki/Jamie_Romeo "Jamie Romeo"), a Democrat.

The county was exclusively governed by a board of supervisors for the first 114 years of its history. In 1935, the position of county manager, appointed by the board, was approved by popular referendum.[[16]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-16) In 1983, the position was replaced by a county executive, directly elected by popular vote, with expanded powers (e.g., veto).[[17]](#cite_note-17) In 1993, the legislature enacted term limits for the executive office of 12 consecutive years to start in 1996.[[18]](#cite_note-Monroe_limits_legislator_terms-18)

#### Sheriff

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sheriff")]

The Monroe County Sheriff's office (MCSO) provides law enforcement and has the constitutional authority to operate the county jail and provide civil functions. As with most counties in New York, the MCSO also performs a range of police services and provides physical and operational security to the courts. The MCSO is led by a [sheriff](/wiki/Sheriff "Sheriff") who is elected by the residents of Monroe County, serving a 4-year term. The sheriff is considered the highest [police](/wiki/Police "Police") official in the county, followed by an appointed [undersheriff](/wiki/Undersheriff "Undersheriff") and subordinate [chief deputy](/wiki/Chief_deputy "Chief deputy").[[19]](#cite_note-19) The Monroe County Sheriff is Todd K. Baxter, a Democrat.

Organizationally, the office is composed of numerous bureaus, each responsible for a given scope of functional operations. The jail bureau is the largest component of the sheriff's office, overseeing an inmate population of around 1,000. Under the New York State Constitution, the sheriff is the [warden](/wiki/Prison_warden "Prison warden") of the county jail.

The police bureau of the sheriff's office operates a sizable road patrol force which serves municipalities within Monroe County that do not independently enforce traffic. They are also responsible for primary police patrols at the [Greater Rochester International Airport](/wiki/Greater_Rochester_International_Airport "Greater Rochester International Airport") and parks throughout the county. Deputies assigned to the marine unit patrol the coastline of [Lake Ontario](/wiki/Lake_Ontario "Lake Ontario") as well as [Irondequoit Bay](/wiki/Irondequoit_Bay "Irondequoit Bay"). The police bureau further employs a [mounted unit](/wiki/Mounted_police "Mounted police"), bomb squad, SWAT team, hostage recovery, criminal investigations, [scuba](/wiki/Scuba_diving "Scuba diving"), and [canine](/wiki/Police_dog "Police dog") units. The court security bureau provides security at the Hall of Justice as well as at the [state appellate court](/wiki/New_York_Court_of_Appeals "New York Court of Appeals") building.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

In 2011, the uniforms were named the 2011 Public Safety Uniform Award in the County Sheriff's/Police Department category by the North American Association of Uniform Manufacturers and Distributors (NAUMD).[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### Legislative branch

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Legislative branch")]

The county's legislative branch consists of a 29-member county legislature which replaced the earlier 43-member board of supervisors on January 1, 1967.[[16]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-16) Members meet in the legislative chambers on the fourth floor of the county office building. All 29 members of the legislature are elected from districts. There are 16 [Democrats](/wiki/Democratic_Party_(United_States) "Democratic Party (United States)") and 13 [Republicans](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)"). The president of the legislature is Yversha Román, a Democrat.[*[as of?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] In 1993, the legislature enacted term limits of 10 consecutive years to start in 1996.[[18]](#cite_note-Monroe_limits_legislator_terms-18) Legislators can return to the office after not being in the legislature for a term. Since the enacting of term limits, as of 2024 four legislators (Stephanie Aldersley, Karla Boyce, Calvin Lee Jr., and Robert Colby) returned after previously leaving due to the term limit; Boyce was reelected again three times; Lee and Colby were appointed to fill vacancies before subsequently being reelected themselves; and Aldersley was appointed before being defeated for reelection.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Economy")]

Monroe County is a home to a number of international businesses, including [Eastman Kodak](/wiki/Eastman_Kodak "Eastman Kodak"),[[23]](#cite_note-23) [Paychex](/wiki/Paychex "Paychex"),[[24]](#cite_note-24) and [Pictometry International](/wiki/Pictometry_International "Pictometry International"),[[25]](#cite_note-25) all of which make Monroe County their world headquarters. While no longer headquartered in Rochester, [Xerox](/wiki/Xerox "Xerox") has its largest manufacturing facilities in Monroe County,[[26]](#cite_note-26) and Bausch and Lomb was headquartered in Rochester until it was acquired by [Valeant Pharmaceuticals](/wiki/Valeant_Pharmaceuticals "Valeant Pharmaceuticals"). Monroe County is also home to regional businesses such as [Wegmans](/wiki/Wegmans "Wegmans"),[[27]](#cite_note-27) Roberts Communications, Inc.,[[28]](#cite_note-28) [Holding Corp.](/wiki/PAETEC_Holding_Corp. "PAETEC Holding Corp."),[[29]](#cite_note-29) and major fashion label [Hickey Freeman](/wiki/Hickey_Freeman "Hickey Freeman").[[30]](#cite_note-30)

### High technology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: High technology")]

Further information: [Tech Valley](/wiki/Tech_Valley "Tech Valley")

[Tech Valley](/wiki/Tech_Valley "Tech Valley"), the technologically recognized area of eastern New York State, has spawned a western offshoot into the Rochester, Monroe County, and [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") areas of New York State. Since the 2000s, as the more established companies in Rochester downsized, the economy of Rochester and Monroe County has been redirected toward [high technology](/wiki/High_technology "High technology"), with new, smaller companies providing the [seed capital](/wiki/Seed_money "Seed money") necessary for business foundation. The Rochester and Monroe County area is important in the field of [photographic processing and imaging](/wiki/Photographic_processing "Photographic processing") as well as [incubating](/wiki/Business_incubator "Business incubator") an increasingly diverse high technology sphere encompassing [STEM fields](/wiki/STEM_fields "STEM fields"), in part the result of private [startup enterprises](/wiki/Startup_company "Startup company") collaborating with major academic institutions, including the [University of Rochester](/wiki/University_of_Rochester "University of Rochester") and [Cornell University](/wiki/Cornell_University "Cornell University").[[31]](#cite_note-31) Given the high prevalence of imaging and [optical science](/wiki/Optical_science "Optical science") among the industry and the universities, Rochester is known as the world capital of imaging. [The Institute of Optics](/wiki/The_Institute_of_Optics "The Institute of Optics") of the [University of Rochester](/wiki/University_of_Rochester "University of Rochester") and the [Rochester Institute of Technology](/wiki/Rochester_Institute_of_Technology "Rochester Institute of Technology") in nearby [Henrietta](/wiki/Henrietta,_New_York "Henrietta, New York") both have imaging programs.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

### Major employers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Major employers")]

Several industries occupy a major portion of the jobs located regionally, with health care comprising a significant portion of jobs in Monroe County. The University of Rochester (including its numerous hospitals) is the largest employer regionally with over 27,000 workers; [Rochester Regional Health](/wiki/Rochester_Regional_Health "Rochester Regional Health") (parent company of Rochester General and Unity hospitals) is the second largest consisting of over 15,000. Wegmans is third with about 13,000 local employees.[[33]](#cite_note-33)

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census") of 2020, there were 759,443 people, 301,948
households, and 232,500 families residing in the county. The population density was 1,155 inhabitants per square mile (446/km2). There were 330,247 housing units at an average density of 502 units per square mile (194 units/km2). The county's racial makeup was 68.6% [White](/wiki/White_American "White American"), 15.7% [African American](/wiki/African_American "African American"), 0.3% [Native American](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States"), 4.3% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_American "Asian American"), 0.01% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), 4.0% from [other races](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census"), and 7.1% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census") or [Latino](/wiki/Race_and_ethnicity_in_the_United_States_Census "Race and ethnicity in the United States Census") of any race were 9.6% of the population. 18.6% were of [Italian](/wiki/Italians "Italians"), 15.3% [German](/wiki/Germans "Germans"), 11.3% [Irish](/wiki/Irish_people "Irish people") and 8.3% [English](/wiki/English_people "English people") ancestry according to [Census 2000](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census"). In 2007, 4.64% of the population reported speaking [Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language") at home, while 1.43% spoke [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language").[[47]](#cite_note-47)

There were 301,948 households, out of which 54% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 18% had a female householder with no husband present, 6% had a male householder with no wife present, and 23% were non-families. The average household size was 2.37.

In the county, the population was spread out, with 21% being 18 or younger, 15% from 19 to 29, 13% from 30 to 39, 11% from 40 to 49, 14% from 50 to 59, 12% from 60 to 69, and 13% who were 70 years of age or older. The median age was 39 years. 52% of the population was [Female](/wiki/Female "Female"), and 48% was [Male](/wiki/Male "Male")

The median income for a household in the county was $62,103. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the county was $35,797. About 12.7% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_threshold "Poverty threshold"), including 19.0% of those under age 18 and 8.0% of those age 65 or over. 90.4% of those 25 years or over was a [High school](/wiki/High_school "High school") graduate or higher, and 38.6% of those 25 years or over had a [Bachelor's degree](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degree "Bachelor's degree") or higher.

According to the U.S. Religion Census of 2020, 380,869 county residents, 50.2% of the county population, adhere to a [Religion](/wiki/Religion "Religion"). Of the 50.2% of Religious adherents, 27.5% (209,584) are [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church"), 9.4% (71,670) are [Protestant](/wiki/Protestantism "Protestantism"), 6.0% (46,140) are [Nondenominational Christians](/wiki/Nondenominational_Christianity "Nondenominational Christianity"), 2.4% (18,648) are [Muslim](/wiki/Islam "Islam"), 1.2% (9,054) are [Hindu](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism"), 1.1% (8,562) are [Jewish](/wiki/Judaism "Judaism"), 0.6% (5,230) are [Jehovah's Witnesses](/wiki/Jehovah%27s_Witnesses "Jehovah's Witnesses"), 0.6% (4,912) are [Mormon](/wiki/The_Church_of_Jesus_Christ_of_Latter-day_Saints "The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints"), 0.5% (4,474) are [Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhist "Buddhist"), and 0.3% (2,595) are [Eastern Orthodox](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodoxy "Eastern Orthodoxy").[[48]](#cite_note-48)

### Primary and secondary education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Primary and secondary education")]

The public school systems educates the overwhelming majority of Monroe County's children.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The schools operated by the [Roman Catholic Diocese of Rochester](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Rochester "Roman Catholic Diocese of Rochester") or [Roman Catholic religious orders](/wiki/Religious_institute_(Catholic) "Religious institute (Catholic)") educate the next largest[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] segment of children, although collectively, they are a distant second.

There are three private schools that serve more than 200 students each:

* [Allendale Columbia School](/wiki/Allendale_Columbia_School "Allendale Columbia School"), a [college preparatory school](/wiki/University-preparatory_school "University-preparatory school") in Pittsford
* [The Harley School](/wiki/The_Harley_School "The Harley School"), a college preparatory school in Brighton
* Mary Cariola Children's Center serving children with multiple, complex disabilities in the city

There is one small, but historically significant school: [Rochester School for the Deaf](/wiki/Rochester_School_for_the_Deaf "Rochester School for the Deaf") in the city

* There are about ten primary schools operated by the [Roman Catholic Diocese of Rochester](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Rochester "Roman Catholic Diocese of Rochester").

### Colleges and universities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Monroe_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Colleges and universities")]

For colleges and universities in the Greater Rochester Area, see [Rochester, New York metropolitan area § Colleges and universities](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York_metropolitan_area#Colleges_and_universities "Rochester, New York metropolitan area").

The county is home to nine colleges and universities:

* [Bryant & Stratton College](/wiki/Bryant_%26_Stratton_College "Bryant & Stratton College") in Greece and Henrietta
* [Colgate Rochester Crozer Divinity School](/wiki/Colgate_Rochester_Crozer_Divinity_School "Colgate Rochester Crozer Divinity School") in the city
* [Monroe Community College](/wiki/Monroe_Community_College "Monroe Community College") in Brighton with a campus in the city
* [Nazareth University](/wiki/Nazareth_University "Nazareth University") in Pittsford
* [Roberts Wesleyan College](/wiki/Roberts_Wesleyan_College "Roberts Wesleyan College") in Chili
* [Rochester Institute of Technology](/wiki/Rochester_Institute_of_Technology "Rochester Institute of Technology") in Henrietta
* [St. Bernard's School of Theology and Ministry](/wiki/St._Bernard%27s_School_of_Theology_and_Ministry "St. Bernard's School of Theology and Ministry") in Pittsford
* [St. John Fisher University](/wiki/St._John_Fisher_College "St. John Fisher College") in Pittsford
* [SUNY Brockport](/wiki/SUNY_Brockport "SUNY Brockport") (also known as the State University of New York Brockport) in Brockport with a campus in Rochester
* [University of Rochester](/wiki/University_of_Rochester "University of Rochester") in Rochester

Additionally, three colleges maintain satellite campuses in Monroe County:

* The [Cornell University School of Industrial and Labor Relations](/wiki/Cornell_University_School_of_Industrial_and_Labor_Relations "Cornell University School of Industrial and Labor Relations") maintains an office in the city[[52]](#cite_note-52)
* [Empire State College](/wiki/Empire_State_College "Empire State College") maintains the Genesee Valley Learning Center in Irondequoit[[53]](#cite_note-53)
* [Ithaca College](/wiki/Ithaca_College "Ithaca College")'s Department of Physical Therapy leases part of the [Colgate Rochester Crozer Divinity School](/wiki/Colgate_Rochester_Crozer_Divinity_School "Colgate Rochester Crozer Divinity School") facility for teaching and research[[54]](#cite_note-54)
